artifact_type: PRD-aligned frontend demo UX + current implementation handoff

mvp.scope: PRD target is a frontend-only demo; current repo is a backend-integrated prototype that implements part of the flow with FastAPI, Redis, SQLite, WebSockets, and LiteLLM.
mvp.primary_goal: Show that a user's agent can meet other agents, find meaningful overlap, and give the user a useful opener.
current_repo_live_implemented: SQLite users, rooms, room participants, and profile versions
Redis matchmaking queue, room state, streams, pub/sub, and arena result cache
LiteLLM-backed agent turns, arena conversations, profile intake, synthesis, and scoring
Google OAuth and dev-login JWT auth
Next.js frontend routes for landing, demo login, arena queue, room conversation, and auth callback
mvp.persistence: PRD target uses localStorage for temporary demo state; current repo uses localStorage for auth/demo client state plus SQLite and Redis for live backend state.
not_yet_implemented_from_prd: event code and mode selection route /join
required playful question UI and capped follow-up flow
PRD-style 5-agent theatrical arena UI by adapting /arena/start, which currently runs against all other database users

not_yet_implemented_backend_capabilities: event code and event mode persistence

current_implementation.summary: The checked-in repo currently runs a live backend-assisted prototype rather than the purely mocked frontend demo described as the PRD build target.

implemented_routes.purpose: Landing page with Try the demo CTA and Google sign-in CTA.
Select one of seven demo personas, call dev-login, store JWT/user/persona state in localStorage, and enter /arena.
Queue the current user through /rooms/matchmake, poll status, show live waiting state, and redirect to /room/[roomId] when matched.
Show live room messages over WebSocket, allow Take Over, Wrap Up, human messages, and display Vibe Score results.

implemented_services: FastAPI application with CORS, sessions, health check, and startup seeding
Google OAuth plus dev-login JWT auth
User profile update and public user read endpoints
Intake endpoint that generates hidden profile YAML, synthesis/matching vector, and system instruction
Room APIs for list, detail, messages, takeover, and completion
WebSocket room stream with Redis Pub/Sub fan-out
Agent conversation loop and post-conversation vibe scoring
Arena API that runs one user against all other database users and returns ranked MatchCard objects

alignment_notes: The PRD target remains a polished, under-3-minute frontend demo with mocked data and hidden YAML.
The current code implements real backend capabilities that were originally listed as future backend work.
The current frontend uses live 1:1 matchmaking and room conversations, not the PRD's PRD-first swipeable match queue and match detail flow.
The backend has an arena API for multi-agent ranked match cards, but the current /arena frontend page is wired to /rooms/matchmake instead of /arena/start.
The PRD describes a short 5-agent arena; /arena/start currently runs against all other users, which means six opponents when the seven seeded demo users exist.
The current intake backend can generate hidden YAML/profile data, but the frontend onboarding and twin preview screens are not implemented.
Generated avatars are currently colored initials, not image assets or real human photos.

routes.name: Landing Page
routes.purpose: Explain Twinder and move users into the demo.

routes.name: Demo Entry
routes.purpose: Show playful twin-building loading, select demo user, and continue as Alexis by default.

routes.name: Join Event
routes.purpose: Let users enter event code, choose mode, or use demo shortcut.

routes.name: Mock Integrations
routes.purpose: Show visual-only options for ChatGPT, Apple Notes, and Upload Files.

routes.name: Intake
routes.purpose: Let users paste context or skip to questions.

routes.name: Required Questions
routes.purpose: Collect enough context to build a usable twin.

routes.name: Twin Preview
routes.purpose: Show a human-readable agent card and get approval.

routes.name: Match Queue
routes.purpose: Show stacked swipeable match cards with explicit actions.

routes.name: Match Detail
routes.purpose: Explain why the match exists and provide an opener.

core_flow: User skips intake or pastes context
User sees match evidence and suggested opener
User can eavesdrop on selected agent conversation
User can ask mocked Copilot why the match is useful
User can Meet, Save, Pass, or Ask Why

required_questions: If you could have an animal follow you around, what kind of animal would it be and why?
What is your favorite color, why, and how does it make you feel?
What do you want from this event/profile, and what should people know about you?
suggested_follow_up_questions: Who are you hoping your agent finds for you?
What can you help people with in 15 minutes?
What should your agent never share?

integrations.behavior: Clicking connect may show a coming soon or mock connected state. No real import occurs.

example_status_feed: Alexis Twin is meeting Haley Twin.
Highlight: both care about making networking less awkward.
Alexis Twin is meeting Leo Twin.
Highlight: agent-to-agent infrastructure meets social UX.
Alexis Twin is meeting Maya Twin.
Highlight: playful product design could make this feel less creepy.
Alexis Twin is meeting Jordan Twin.
Highlight: community launch strategy could make the demo spread.

fields: what you can help them with
what they can help you with
actions: Give me a less awkward opener

meet_action.example_copy: Meet request saved. In the real app, Haley would be notified that you are interested in meeting.

preview_prompts: Make this sound more like me
match_detail_prompts: Why should I meet this person?
Give me a less awkward opener

mode_language.Networking: professional relevance, mutual help, warm intro
mode_language.Dating: compatibility, emotional style, values, conversation chemistry

displays: one focused question at a time

feel: less corporate than typical AI tools
avoid: overly technical language on user-facing screens

core_flow: User can paste context if desired
User can answer required playful questions
User can answer capped follow-up questions
User can preview and approve twin
User can eavesdrop on agent conversations
User can get mocked Copilot explanation
Meet opens a mock confirmation screen
data: Match copy is specific and compelling
ui: No raw debug JSON in normal flow
Demo can be completed in under 3 minutes

resolved_alignment_changes: Replaced dashboard-first live-event MVP with PRD's frontend demo flow
Replaced older backend and observability assumptions with mocked frontend-only scope
Replaced no-photo language with generated-avatar demo language while still excluding real human photos
Replaced intro-request/mutual-intro MVP mechanics with mocked Meet confirmation
Added PRD routes, arena, match queue, match detail, Eavesdrop, and mock Copilot surfaces
Removed outdated open ambiguities that conflicted with PRD decisions
Added current repo implementation snapshot showing backend-integrated prototype state and explicit gaps against the PRD frontend-demo target
